hero.name: Vernet Adjobi
hero.title: BI Analyst & AI Builder
hero.subtitle: I turn data into actionable dashboards and build AI agents that automate real business processes.

about.title: About
about.p1: Research Assistant at the PRIME lab (Université de Moncton), working on the application of LLMs and Vision-Language Models for clinical decision support. My current project, MedCouncil, is a fully sovereign multi-agent system for clinical diagnosis.
about.p2: My background combines BI rigor — internships at UNI Financial Cooperation — with progressive immersion in AI research, first at Polytechnique Montréal's SWAT Lab, now at PRIME.
about.p3: I'm also building lambdaTools, an early-stage AI agency to bring automation to SMBs.

jobs.desc: Applying LLMs, SLMs, and Vision-Language Models to medical diagnosis assistance. Building MedCouncil — a fully sovereign multi-agent clinical decision support system.
Selected from 301 applicants across 36 Canadian universities to explore the application of LLMs and RAG techniques for automated software vulnerability detection and repair.

Developed technical documentation to standardize and accelerate OSFI regulatory reporting. Bridged raw data and compliance requirements by structuring data definitions, query logic, and reporting workflows used by the finance team.
Database refactoring and query optimization on SQL Server production environment. Redesigned table structures and indexing strategies, achieving 2x query performance improvement over 4 months.

items.title: MedCouncil
items.desc: Sovereign multi-agent clinical decision support system. 3 specialist urgency agents (MedGemma 4B) + 1 reasoning orchestrator (DeepSeek-R1). +26.7% top-3 accuracy in emergency medicine on 1,500 DDXPlus cases. 100% local, zero commercial APIs.

items.title: lambdaTools
items.desc: Early-stage AI agency for SMB automation. Designing custom LangGraph agents for workflow automation, API integrations, and productivity gains that actually ship.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: A project, an opportunity, a question?
contact.availability: Open to freelance mandates, projects etc ... just hit me up !

acknowledgements.title: Acknowledgements
acknowledgements.subtitle: To those who trusted me before the CV was impressive.

institutions.name: PRIME
institutions.desc: Thank you to the PRIME lab, Université de Moncton, and Prof. Akhloufi for welcoming me as a Research Assistant, mentoring me, and guiding the development of MedCouncil.

institutions.name: SWAT Lab
institutions.desc: Thank you to the SWAT Lab, Prof. Khomh, and Polytechnique Montréal for selecting me from 301 applicants to join the team. This first immersion into AI research was a turning point in my trajectory.
institutions.personTitle: SWAT Director, Canada Research Chair, IEEE Fellow

institutions.name: UNI Financial Cooperation
institutions.desc: Thank you to UNI for welcoming me twice — first in BI, then in Data Governance. This experience grounded my skills in a real, demanding business environment.
